settings.accordion_subtitle: Discover our streamlined process designed to deliver exceptional results with personalized care.
settings.accordion_question_1: How do I book a consultation?
settings.accordion_answer_1:
Simply click on any service above and select your preferred time slot. Our team will contact you within 24 hours to confirm your appointment and discuss your specific needs.
Choosing your practitioner —
How should I decide between the two practitioners?

Pick by fit, not seniority. Choose Core Wellness Consult if your goals are focused—stress, sleep, energy, routine resets—and you want practical steps you can start this week. Choose Advanced Case Consult if your history is complex, you’ve tried several approaches, or you want deeper systems mapping and strategy.

Why are the prices different?

Pricing reflects session scope and practitioner expertise. The Advanced Case option includes more analysis time and strategic planning. Both options follow the same care standards and deliver personalized, actionable guidance.

Is the lower-priced consult “lite” care?

No. Core Wellness is designed for clarity and momentum. You’ll receive a targeted plan, a concise recap, and access to our wellness shop at preferred pricing so you can choose only what you need—at a lower cost.

Can I switch practitioners later?

Yes. You can begin with Core Wellness and transition to an Advanced Case consult if your needs evolve. Your notes and priorities carry forward so you never start over.

Do the practitioners collaborate?

Yes. Plans are developed to shared standards, and complex cases may be peer-reviewed internally. You benefit from a unified approach, whichever consult you book.

What does each session include?

Core Wellness Consult: a focused session aimed at quick wins, clear next steps, and habit support—ideal for foundational goals.

Advanced Case Consult: an in-depth session with systems review, strategic phasing, and comprehensive plan mapping—ideal for multi-factor or long-standing concerns.

What if I’m still unsure?

When in doubt, start with Core Wellness. It’s designed to establish momentum quickly. If your case proves more complex, we’ll recommend an Advanced Case consult and adjust your plan seamlessly.
settings.accordion_question_2: What happens during the initial consultation?
settings.accordion_answer_2:
Guided by one of our experts, your first session with the bio-survey will create a Baseline from a sequence of communication with several Bio-Markers.
What to expect in your first session

Guided by one of our experts, your first bio-survey establishes a baseline by measuring how your body communicates with several bioenergetic markers.

Intake & goals: We review your goals, history, and preferences to focus the scan.

Baseline reading: You rest your hand on the ZYTO® hand cradle. It measures Galvanic Skin Response (GSR)—tiny changes in skin conductance that reflect moment-to-moment nervous-system activity.

Digital “stressors” library: The system presents a curated set of virtual items (vitamins, herbs, therapies, body systems, and lifestyle modalities) refined over 20+ years.

Real-time feedback: With each item, the cradle records minute shifts in GSR—think of it as your body “voting” on whether that item is energetically coherent (supportive) right now.

Prioritization: The software ranks the items your body responded to most clearly and groups them into themes (for example, foundational support, stress response, digestion, recovery).

Plan design: We translate those priorities into a practical plan—targeted nutrients or botanicals, simple habits, and supportive therapies—organized into clear phases and timelines.

What you receive

A concise report highlighting your top priority items and themes.

Action steps with suggested timing (and dosing where appropriate) plus lifestyle guidance.

Wellness shop access: You’ll have a curated selection of high-quality supplements and self-care tools at preferred pricing, so you can choose only what you need—at a lower cost—to support your plan.
settings.accordion_question_3: How are the treatment plans customized?
settings.accordion_answer_3:
How we create your personalized plan

We use non-invasive biofeedback to see how your body’s nervous system responds to digital “questions.” Those questions are virtual items—supplements, body systems, and lifestyle factors—presented by the ZYTO® system. Your plan is built from what your body appears to recognize as most supportive.

Note: Biofeedback is not a medical diagnosis or treatment. It’s wellness guidance that complements, not replaces, care from your licensed healthcare provider.
settings.accordion_question_4: What on going support do you provide?
settings.accordion_answer_4:
Ongoing support & follow-up consults

Your plan isn’t static—your body, stress load, and schedule evolve. Follow-up consults keep your guidance aligned with what’s happening now, so small course-corrections prevent plateaus and help you progress with confidence.

At each follow-up, we review what’s changed since your last session, revisit goals, and run a focused bio-survey comparison against your baseline. We then refine your plan—adjusting nutrients, timing, and lifestyle steps; simplifying where needed; and pacing changes so they’re realistic.

Most clients benefit from a steady rhythm for check-ins—often every four to six weeks—long enough to implement updates yet close enough to catch shifts early. This cadence builds momentum, keeps recommendations cost-effective, and ensures your plan grows with you.
settings.accordion_question_5: How does the Biofeedback Screening work
settings.accordion_answer_5:
A deeper look at how the ZYTO® biofeedback scan works

ZYTO uses a non-invasive form of biofeedback called Galvanic Skin Response (GSR) to see how your autonomic nervous system reacts to a series of digital “questions.” Each question is a virtual item—a software representation of a supplement, body system, lifestyle factor, or other wellness marker. Your moment-to-moment responses help prioritize what may be most supportive for you right now.

The signal it measures (GSR)

What it is: GSR tracks tiny changes in how easily a gentle electrical signal passes across the skin (skin conductance).

Why it matters: These changes reflect short-term shifts in the sympathetic branch of your nervous system—similar to how heart rate varies when you focus, relax, or respond to stimuli.

What it is not: It doesn’t diagnose disease, read thoughts, or measure nutrient levels. It’s a stress-response signal, not a lab test.

What “virtual items” are

In the software, each virtual item is a standardized digital signature used to pose a consistent question to your nervous system (for example, “How does your system respond to this botanical?” or “this body-system theme?”). In plain terms, they’re structured prompts that let the system compare your reactions across a large, curated library.

The scan loop (what happens during a session)

Baseline: You rest your hand on the ZYTO hand cradle while the system records a short baseline of your natural skin conductance.

Present a prompt: The software introduces one virtual item at a time.

Measure the change: The cradle samples GSR for a brief window and looks for a small, time-locked deviation from your baseline.

Stability checks: If the signal is noisy (movement, very dry skin, and similar factors), the system retries or flags the reading.

Log and continue: That item’s response is saved, then the process moves to the next item.

Normalize over time: Periodically, the baseline is refreshed to account for gradual drift from temperature, hydration, or relaxation as the session progresses.

How results are scored

Direction: Minute increases or decreases from baseline are interpreted as relative “recognition” or “resistance” to that item at this moment.

Magnitude: Larger, cleaner deviations generally receive higher confidence scores.

Prioritization: Items are ranked by coherence with your responses and grouped into themes such as foundational support, stress response, digestion, or recovery.

Quality and reliability factors

Contact and hydration: Clean, consistent hand contact and normal hydration improve signal quality.

Environment: Comfortable temperature, minimal movement, and reduced distractions help reduce noise.

Consistency: Repeat scans under similar conditions make comparisons more meaningful over time.

What the results mean (and don’t mean)

They reflect your body’s momentary stress-response patterns to structured prompts—useful for wellness guidance and prioritization.

They do not diagnose, treat, or cure any medical condition, and they are not a substitute for medical evaluation or lab testing.

settings.expert_1_description: Discover Mike Payne at Living Well Today – Your Expert in Holistic Wellness. With 25+ years' experience, Mike specializes in complex health issues like autoimmune conditions & neurodegenerative disorders.
settings.expert_1_bio:
At Living Well Today, Mike Payne leverages over 25 years of dedicated experience in holistic wellness to guide individuals towards optimal health and vitality. Renowned for his expertise in managing complex health issues, Mike specializes in treating autoimmune conditions, neurodegenerative disorders, Lyme diseased .

As a trailblazer in alternative health and functional medicine. His comprehensive approach addresses a wide range of complex conditions, including autoimmune complications, Lyme disease, various cancers, spectrum disorders, and neurodegenerative diseases.

Mike's commitment to holistic wellness goes beyond merely treating symptoms; he delves into the root causes of health issues to develop personalized, effective treatment plans. His innovative methods and compassionate care have earned him a reputation as a leading expert in the field.
settings.expert_2_description: Elevate Your Well-Being with Aaron Payne at Living Well Today International. With 15+ years of expertise. Explore neurofeedback, and more for personalized care for general wellness. Start your journey today.
settings.expert_2_bio:
Aaron is a dedicated specialist with extensive experience in autism spectrum support. A graduate of Marshall University with a degree in Sports Medicine, Aaron has spent his career combining medical expertise with a deep understanding of neurodivergent care.

Recognized for his unwavering commitment, Aaron has been nominated by Autism One as one of America's top autism practitioners. His compassionate approach and evidence-based strategies have helped countless individuals and families navigate the complexities of autism with confidence and clarity.

Through personalized consultations, Aaron provides expert guidance on interventions, therapies, and lifestyle strategies tailored to each individual’s needs. Whether you're seeking support for autism-related challenges or looking to optimize health and performance through sports medicine, Aaron’s knowledge and dedication make him an invaluable resource.
